text: Abstract : Purpose: This research was aimed at finding out the effects of text-rich environment on children’s language acquisition implemented in an early childhood education institution called PAUD. Design/Methodology/Approach: Qualitative case study as a research methodology was employed in this study. Data were collected by observing the whole class activities in a PAUD Shafa Marwah institution, conducting interviews with the educators and the parents of the students concerned, and collecting documents about the institution. Findings: Analyzing the obtained data by using Qualitative Analysis (NVivo) software showed that teaching and learning processes became more enjoyable by adding various textual contents to the classroom environment. By adding various types of texts to the environment—ranging from literature that children read from storybooks and posters to the ones that educators hang on the boards—the children become much more interested in reading activities and are motivated to communicate linguistically more frequently. So the text rich classroom environment improved children’s language acquisition in several ways such as; increased their wordbooks, helped to recognize the text in the stories they read, developed their speaking fluently skills and stimulated the children in an early way of literacy and communication. Originality/Value: Text rich environment which was implemented on students in the study setting, contributed a lot towards the children’s learning and increasing the effective communication, at the same time the text rich environment helped towards the children’s cognitive skills towards reading, writing, storytelling and helped in the children’s understanding of vocabulary.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
